chunks.text: HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P No. D-932 of 2015 Present: Justice Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry Justice Riazat Ali Sahar Petitioner: Zulfiqar through Mr. Muhammad Suleman Unar, Advocate. Respondents: Through Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri, Assistant Advocate General, Sindh. Date of order: 31.03.2026 O R D E R Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry J. - Petitioner submits that he was serving the Sindh Workers Welfare Board [SWWB] in BPS-16 when he applied for the post of Administrator in BPS-18 and was issued an appointment order dated 31-12-2008 on ad-hoc basis; that he submitted joining letter the same day; however, right thereafter, the Director General of the SWWB took back the original letter of appointment from the Petitioner and informed him verbally that he stood terminated from the post. The Petitioner’s prayer is for reinstatement to the post of Administrator SWWB. On the other hand, written statement of the SWWB is that the Petitioner was never appointed to the post of Administrator SWWB; that the appointment order dated 31-12-2008 produced by him is a fabricated document; and that the joining letter also does not exist in the record of the SWWB. Though the appointment order relied upon by the Petitioner is supposedly signed by the Secretary of the SWWB, it does not bear any seal/stamp of the Board, it does not mention the outward number of the letter, and does it not mention the appointing authority. Admittedly, the Petitioner is not in possession of the original appointment order. The Petitioner also does not explain why he waited for 6 years before filing the petition. These facts tend to support the submission that the appointment order is not genuine.

The Petitioner also does not explain why he waited for 6 years before filing the petition. These facts tend to support the submission that the appointment order is not genuine. Nevertheless, the question raised to the authenticity of the appointment order is a question of fact that cannot be addressed in constitutional jurisdiction. The petition is therefore dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE *Hafiz Fahad*
